text.title: Learning Speed as Competitive Moat Examples
text.summary: Worked end-to-end example showing the methodology applied on a representative case.

example.title: Quarterly belief-audit drives a bet kill on confidence drop
example.code: audit_period: 2026-Q2 beliefs: - id: b-mid-market-msp statement: Mid-market MSPs will pay for usage-based tier. confidence: 0.32 last_update: 2026-05-10 evidence_type: experiment stale_flag: false decision_velocity: p0_avg_days: 8.2 p1_avg_days: 2.1 p2_avg_days: 0.3 decisions_this_quarter: 47 loss_attribution: losses_total: 6 by_cause: {shipped-sooner: 3, wrong-belief: 2, better-evidence: 1} kill_criteria_present: true outcome: bet 'usage-based MSP tier' killed; capacity redirected to enterprise-NFR work

example.title: Strategy doc citing 2024 evidence; sunk-cost bet preserved
example.code: audit_period: 2026-Q2 beliefs: - id: b1 statement: customers love it confidence: 0.95 last_update: 2024-09-01 evidence_type: intuition decision_velocity: {p0_avg_days: 42, p1_avg_days: 18, p2_avg_days: 4} loss_attribution: {losses_total: 6, by_cause: {}} kill_criteria_present: false
example.note: Illustrates implicit-beliefs (intuition) + stale-evidence (2024) + missing-loss-codes + sunk-cost continuation.
